metadata.title: Why an agent with fresh memory every session still knows exactly where it left off
metadata.purpose: Move state out of the context window into two durable artifacts — `feature_list.json` (source of truth, per-feature pass/fail) and git history (immutable record); each session reads, does one, tests, commits, marks.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: git, test suites, context limits, session persistence
metadata.exclusions: how the initial feature list is generated, testing-framework internals
metadata.visual_object: A `feature_list.json` file checking rows off across vertical session-boundary dividers
metadata.hook: An agent with 200 features to build gets a blank context each session — yet it can't afford to re-read the whole codebase or repeat finished work.

beats.narration_text: Session 1 implements features 1–50, marks each passing in `feature_list.json`, commits. Session 2 boots with zero conversation history, reads the file, sees 51–200 still incomplete, and starts at 51 — no re-derivation.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If the agent loses all in-context memory between sessions, how does it resume without repeating finished features or losing track of what remains?
An agent with 200 features to build gets a blank context each session — yet it can't afford to re-read the whole codebase or repeat finished work.

Move state out of the context window into two durable artifacts — `feature_list.json` (source of truth, per-feature pass/fail) and git history (immutable record); each session reads, does one, tests, commits, marks.
beats.visual: A `feature_list.json` file checking rows off across vertical session-boundary dividers
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: 200 items all "incomplete"; session 1 → 1–50 "passing"; session 2 reads, resumes at 51, ends with 51–100 "passing".

Move state out of the context window into two durable artifacts — `feature_list.json` (source of truth, per-feature pass/fail) and git history (immutable record); each session reads, does one, tests, commits, marks.
